text.title: Fine-tune cost vs prompt decision — core rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every decision record must satisfy.

rule.statement: The record states an explicit `lift_bar` (minimum Δeval-score that would justify fine-tuning, e.g. +0.05). The product or eng owner sets it BEFORE seeing any fine-tune result.
rule.rationale: Setting the bar after the experiment is post-hoc rationalisation. Pre-committed bars block "well it's a little better" sunk-cost arguments.

rule.statement: Break-even calculation is shown in the record: `(training_cost + ops_overhead) / (Δ$/k * daily_volume * 30)` = months to recoup. If break-even > 12 months, fine-tune is rejected unless another rule provides override.
rule.rationale: Without break-even arithmetic, hosting and ops costs are invisible until month 4, when the team is committed.

rule.statement: Fine-tune recommendation requires ≥2 of 5 strong signals: (a) format/style adherence problem prompt cannot solve, (b) latency-critical workload where a smaller fine-tuned model wins, (c) data volume ≥ 5,000 high-quality examples, (d) safety/policy domain where prompt instructions leak, (e) per-call cost > per-call training amortised within 12 months. Fewer than 2 → prompt+RAG path.
rule.rationale: Single-signal arguments (just data volume, just cost) repeatedly mis-route teams into low-ROI training runs.

rule.statement: A named human owner signs the record (`owner` field, not "team"). Auto-generated records without a named owner are rejected.
rule.rationale: Decisions without an owner orphan when the team changes; nobody re-evaluates.

rule.statement: Record declares `recheck_at` (ISO date ≤ 6 months from `created_at`). When the date passes, the record is stale and must be revisited.
rule.rationale: Model pricing and capability shift quarterly; a 2-year-old fine-tune decision is almost always wrong by month 13.

rule.statement: All five rules satisfied: lift_bar set, break-even computed, ≥2 strong signals (or explicit no-FT recommendation), owner named, recheck_at within 6 months.
No baseline eval OR research-only context OR compliance forces FT — record cannot be authored meaningfully.
